steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says certain features of mosque architecture are "nearly ______" and then immediately clarifies with "which almost all mosques include." The blank must mean something close to "found in all cases" or "present everywhere" — describing how widespread the feature is, not how complex or ideal it is.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — universal: Universal means occurring or found everywhere, in all cases. The passage's own gloss ("which almost all mosques include") is a near-synonym for universal, making this an exact fit. ✓
  • Choice B — elaborate: Elaborate means detailed or complex. The passage says nothing about the complexity or intricacy of these features — only how commonly they appear. ✗
  • Choice C — illusory: Illusory means based on illusion or deceptive. Nothing in the passage suggests these features are false or misleading. ✗
  • Choice D — idealized: Idealized means regarded as perfect or better than reality. The passage is making a descriptive point about prevalence, not a normative claim about quality or perfection. ✗
A is correct.
